Title: The Diary of a Young Girl
Author: Anne Frank

Title: Thinking in Bets: Making Smarter Decisions When You Don't Have All the Facts
Author: Annie Duke

Title: Green Island
Author: Shawna Yang Ryan
My Review: reading for masters. this book was heartbreakingly delightful. lots to be explored about how the eastern and westen views of taiwanese people differ and the concept of orphanhood in cultural identity. massive shade thrown at the oxford study.

Title: Orphan of Asia (Modern Chinese Literature from Taiwan)
Author: Wu Zhuoliu
My Review: read it for masters. its hard to find books that discuss colonial taiwan but i felt like this book did a good job expanding on the idea of the complications of multicultural identity.

Title: Fahrenheit 451
Author: Ray Bradbury
My Review: one of my favorite books of all time. this book was written in the basement of powell library at UCLA and i had the honor and pleasure of sitting where it was written to read it. 10/10

Title: Grit: The Power of Passion and Perseverance
Author: Angela Duckworth

Title: Free Food for Millionaires
Author: Min Jin Lee
My Review: A good read for a postgrad like me. Good lessons, insight, and commentary on corporate hierarchy, growing up, and love and relationships. I felt slightly disappointed by the ending and overall felt there was not a ton of character development.
